linux: While not having the Revolution Pi board powered, connect your system to the board's USB Console / Service port via a micro-USB cable.
Power on the Revolution Pi board (24 V DC).
Write the OS to the internal eMMC. We recommend using [Etcher](http://www.etcher.io/) or `dd` with care.
When flashing is complete, power off the board and unplug the micro-USB cable.
Connect Ethernet on the X1 RJ45 and power the board on.

windows: While not having the Revolution Pi board powered, connect your system to the board's USB Console / Service port via a micro-USB cable.
Power on the Revolution Pi board (24 V DC).
Write the OS to the internal eMMC. We recommend using [Etcher](http://www.etcher.io/).
When flashing is complete, power off the board and unplug the micro-USB cable.
Connect Ethernet on the X1 RJ45 and power the board on.

bootDevice: Apply power to the Revolution Pi Core SE.
connectDevice: Connect the Core SE's micro-USB Console / Service port to your host using a USB data cable.
disconnectDevice: When flashing is complete, power off the Core SE and unplug the micro-USB cable.
